name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا مِن قَبْلِكَ فِى شِيَعِ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ (١٠)
```

```english
Certainly We sent [apostles] before you to former communities, (10)
```

```arabic
وَمَا يَأْتِيهِم مِّن رَّسُولٍ إِلَّا كَانُوا۟ بِهِۦ يَسْتَهْزِءُونَ (١١)
```

```english
and there did not come to them any apostle but that they used to deride him. (11)
```

```arabic
كَذَٰلِكَ نَسْلُكُهُۥ فِى قُلُوبِ ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ (١٢)
```

```english
That is how We let it pass through the hearts of the guilty: (12)
```

```arabic
لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِهِۦ ۖ وَقَدْ خَلَتْ سُنَّةُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ (١٣)
```

```english
they do not believe in it, and the precedent of the ancients has already passed. (13)
```

```arabic
وَلَوْ فَتَحْنَا عَلَيْهِم بَابًا مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ فَظَلُّوا۟ فِيهِ يَعْرُجُونَ (١٤)
```

```english
Were We to open for them a gate of the sky, so that they could go on ascending through it, (14)
```

```arabic
لَقَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّمَا سُكِّرَتْ أَبْصَـٰرُنَا بَلْ نَحْنُ قَوْمٌ مَّسْحُورُونَ (١٥)
```

```english
they would surely say, ‘Indeed a spell has been cast on our eyes; indeed, we are a bewitched lot.’ (15)
```

## (Commentary)

After mentioning their mockery of His Book and His Prophet, and their demand that he bring forth the angels as a sign (الآية) of his messengership, He followed this with three groups of verses. These are introduced by His saying: **وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنا مِنْ قَبْلِكَ** (And certainly We sent before you...) etc.; His saying: **وَلَقَدْ جَعَلْنا فِي السَّماءِ بُرُوجاً** (And certainly We have set constellations in the sky...) etc.; and His saying: **وَلَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا الْإِنْسانَ مِنْ صَلْصالٍ** (And certainly We created man from a dry clay...) etc.

In the first group, He explains that this mockery is the habit and established practice (السنة الجارية) of the guilty (المجرمين), and that they would not believe even if every single sign came to them. In the second, He shows that there are celestial and terrestrial signs sufficient for those granted the success (التوفيق) to believe. In the third, He explains that the divergence between belief (الإيمان) and disbelief (الكفر) within the human species, and the straying of the people of misguidance, was something determined for them on the day Allah initiated the creation of man—when He created Adam and there occurred what occurred regarding the command to the angels to prostrate and Iblis’s refusal to do so.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنا مِنْ قَبْلِكَ فِي شِيَعِ الْأَوَّلِينَ** **Certainly We sent [messengers] before you among the sects of the ancients.** (15:10) until the end of the two verses. The word *shiya‘* (الشيع) is the plural of *shi‘ah* (شيعة), which refers to a group united upon a tradition (سنة) or a school of thought (مذهب) that they follow. The Exalted said: **مِنَ الَّذِينَ فَرَّقُوا دِينَهُمْ وَكانُوا شِيَعاً كُلُّ حِزْبٍ بِما لَدَيْهِمْ فَرِحُونَ** **of those who split up their religion and became sects, each party exulting in what it had.** (30:32).

His saying: **وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنا** (And certainly We sent) implies "messengers," which has been omitted because it is understood. The focus is on the fundamental occurrence of sending from before, without regard to who was sent. Rather, it is to demonstrate that the earlier humans, like the later ones, were accustomed to disrespecting the divine message, mocking whoever brought it, and persisting in their guilt (إجرامهم). This serves as a consolation to the Prophet صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله so that his breast does not feel straitened by the denial and mockery they confront him with, as He will return to this at the end of the Surah in His saying: **وَلَقَدْ نَعْلَمُ أَنَّكَ يَضِيقُ صَدْرُكَ بِما يَقُولُونَ** **We certainly know that your breast is straitened by what they say.** (15:97).

The meaning is: Be at peace, for We have sent down the Reminder (الذكر) — *the divine revelation that serves as a source of admonition and remembrance* — upon you and We shall preserve it. Let not your breast be straitened by what they say, for it is the habit of the guilty among the human nations. I swear, We certainly sent before you among the factions and sects of the ancients, and their condition was such that: **وَمَا يَأْتِيهِمْ مِنْ رَسُولٍ إِلَّا كَانُوا بِهِ يَسْتَهْزِئُونَ** **There did not come to them any apostle but they used to deride him.** (15:11).

**His saying, the Exalted**: **كَذلِكَ نَسْلُكُهُ فِي قُلُوبِ الْمُجْرِمِينَ** **Thus do We let it pass through the hearts of the guilty:** (15:12) until the end of the two verses. *Al-Suluk* (السلوك) means penetration (النفاذ) or causing to pass through (الإنفاذ). It is said: "He traversed (سلك) the path," meaning he passed through it; and "He threaded (سلك) the string through the needle," meaning he caused it to pass through and inserted it. Linguists mention that *salaka* (سلك) and *aslaka* (أسلك) share the same meaning.

The two pronouns in **نَسْلُكُهُ** (We let it pass) and **بِهِ** (in it) refer to the "Reminder" (الذكر) mentioned previously, which is the Noble Quran. The meaning is that the state of your message and your call through the Reminder sent down to you resembles the state of the messages before you. Just as We sent before you and they met it with rejection and mockery, so too do We insert this Reminder and cause it to penetrate the hearts of these guilty ones, while We know regarding it: **لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِهِ وَقَدْ خَلَتْ سُنَّةُ الْأَوَّلِينَ** **they do not believe in it, and the precedent of the ancients has already passed.** (15:13). Their way and tradition (سنة) was to mock the truth and not follow it. Thus, these two verses are close in meaning to His saying: **فَما كانُوا لِيُؤْمِنُوا بِما كَذَّبُوا بِهِ مِنْ قَبْلُ** **But they would not believe in what they had concluded to be a lie before.** (7:101).

It has been suggested that the two pronouns refer to polytheism (الشرك) or mockery (الاستهزاء) understood from the preceding verses, and that the preposition *bi* (بـ) in **بِهِ** denotes causality (السببية). The meaning would then be: "Thus do We cause polytheism or mockery to penetrate the hearts of the guilty; they do not believe because of polytheism or mockery," etc.

However, this is a remote meaning. What immediately comes to mind from the phrase **لا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِهِ** (they do not believe in it) is that the preposition *bi* is for transitivity (التعدية) rather than causality.

It has also been suggested that the first pronoun refers to mockery understood from the preceding context, while the second refers to the previously mentioned Reminder. The meaning would be: "Just as We caused mockery to pass through the hearts of the sects of the ancients, We cause mockery to pass through and penetrate the hearts of these guilty ones; they do not believe in the Reminder," etc.

There is no harm in this interpretation, even if it necessitates a distinction between two consecutive pronouns. The fact that the phrase **لا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِهِ** (they do not believe in it) resists having its pronoun refer back to "mockery" is sufficient evidence for this distinction.

Furthermore, the objection raised against these two views—that referring the pronoun of **نَسْلُكُهُ** (We let it pass) to mockery implies that the polytheists are compelled (ملجئين) toward polytheism and forced (مجبرين) into it—does not hold.

The reason this objection is invalid is that the Exalted conditioned this "passing through" (السلوك) upon their being "guilty" (المجرمين). This implies they were already characterized by guilt before the act of "passing through" was performed upon them. Thus, it falls under the category of divine misguidance (الإضلال الإلهي) as a recompense (مجازاة), which is not problematic. What is prohibited is initial misguidance (الإضلال الابتدائي), for which there is no evidence in the verse; rather, the evidence is to the contrary. The verse is similar to His saying, the Exalted: **يُضِلُّ بِهِ كَثِيراً وَيَهْدِي بِهِ كَثِيراً وَما يُضِلُّ بِهِ إِلَّا الْفاسِقِينَ** **He misleads many by it and guides many by it, and He misleads no one by it except the transgressors.** (2:26). A detailed discussion on this has preceded.

It has become clear from the foregoing that what is meant by "the tradition of the ancients" (سنة الأولين) is the tradition established by the ancients themselves, not the tradition Allah established regarding the ancients. Thus, the tradition is theirs, not Allah's tradition concerning them—as some commentators have mentioned. This is more appropriate for the context of censuring them and consoling him صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله by mentioning their rejection and mockery of their messengers.

**His saying, the Exalted**: **وَلَوْ فَتَحْنا عَلَيْهِمْ باباً مِنَ السَّماءِ فَظَلُّوا فِيهِ يَعْرُجُونَ لَقالُوا إِنَّما سُكِّرَتْ أَبْصارُنا** **Even if We open to them a gate in the sky, and they were to keep ascending through it, they would surely say, ‘Our eyes have only been dazzled;** (15:14-15) etc. *Al-‘Uruj* (العروج) in the sky means ascending to it, and *al-taskir* (التسكير) means a covering or blurring (الغشاوة).

The meaning of opening a gate from the sky to them is the creation of a path by which it becomes possible for them to enter the higher world (العالم العلوي), which is the abode of the angels. It is not, as some might imagine, a physical ceiling (سقف جرماني) with a two-leafed door that opens and closes. The Exalted has said: **فَفَتَحْنا أَبْوابَ السَّماءِ بِماءٍ مُنْهَمِرٍ** **Then We opened the gates of the sky with pouring water.** (54:11).

The Glorified chose, from among the miracles (الخوارق) thought to remove doubt and eliminate suspicion from their souls, the opening of a gate from the sky and their ascension through it, because this was greater in their eyes than anything else. For this reason, when they proposed great miracles to him, they mentioned ascending into the sky at the end of those miracles in a climactic order, as Allah recounted from them in His saying: **وَقالُوا لَنْ نُؤْمِنَ لَكَ حَتَّى تَفْجُرَ لَنا مِنَ الْأَرْضِ يَنْبُوعاً** **They say, ‘We will not believe you until you make a spring gush forth from the ground for us,** until He said: **أَوْ تَرْقى فِي السَّماءِ وَلَنْ نُؤْمِنَ لِرُقِيِّكَ حَتَّى تُنَزِّلَ عَلَيْنا كِتاباً نَقْرَؤُهُ** **or you ascend into the sky, and we will not believe your ascension until you bring down to us a book that we may read.’** (17:90-93). Thus, ascending into the sky and acting within its affairs—such as bringing down a readable book from it—meaning the penetration of a human into the higher world and his empowerment within and over it, was the most wondrous of miracles to them.

Furthermore, the sky is the abode of the noble angels and the place from which divine decrees and commands issue. Within it are the Tablets of Destiny (ألواح التقادير), through it the courses of affairs run, it is the source of revelation (الوحي), and to it the records of deeds ascend. Thus, a human's ascension through it necessitates his acquaintance with the courses of affairs, the causes of miracles, and the realities of revelation, prophethood, the call, felicity, and wretchedness. In short, it necessitates his oversight of every reality—especially if it were a continuous ascension rather than a single, sudden one, as indicated by His saying: **فَظَلُّوا فِيهِ يَعْرُجُونَ** (and they were to keep ascending through it), where He used the expression **فَظَلُّوا** (they continued/remained) rather than simply saying "they ascended through it."

Opening and ascending in this manner would acquaint them with the foundations and roots of this true call. However, due to the corruption in their hearts and the filth of deep-seated doubt and suspicion in their souls, they would deem their own eyes mistaken in what they witness; rather, they would accuse the Prophet صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله of having bewitched them, and that they are a people under his spell.

The meaning is: Even if We were to open a gate from the sky for them and facilitate their entry into its world, and they continued to ascend through it, ascension after ascension, until their witnessing of the secrets of the Unseen (الغيب) and the dominion (ملكوت) of things was repeated, they would surely say: "Our eyes have only been covered, so they witnessed things that have no reality; rather, we are a people bewitched."
